steps.title: Rewrite using exponent rules
steps.content:
To match the target form with exponent $x$, rewrite the fractional exponent by pulling it from the base:
$$f(x) = (1.84)^{x/4} = \left((1.84)^{1/4}\right)^x$$
Now both sides are expressed as a base raised to power $x$.

steps.title: Equate the bases
steps.content:
Since $\left((1.84)^{1/4}\right)^x = \left(1 + \frac{p}{100}\right)^x$ and the exponents match, the bases must be equal:
$$(1.84)^{1/4} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the fourth root and solve for p
steps.content:
Calculate the fourth root of 1.84:
$$(1.84)^{1/4} \approx 1.164$$
(Verify: $1.164^4 \approx 1.84$.) Substitute into the equation:
$$1.164 = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$
$$0.164 = \frac{p}{100}$$
$$p = 16.4$$
Among the four choices, 16.4 is closest to 16. That matches choice A — A is correct.
